extracted.brandBackground: West Coast Sourdough operates a fast casual restaurant franchise system serving specialty sandwiches, soups, and salads.

leadership.name: Manjinder Deol
leadership.background: Served as CFO, Director, and Secretary of WCSD, Inc. from 2020 to 2024, and has managed West Coast Sourdough restaurants since 2018.
leadership.whyItMatters: He has direct operational and executive experience with the brand since its inception.

leadership.name: Damon Bhatia
leadership.background: Served as Director of WCSD, Inc. from 2020 to 2023, and was Assistant Manager of a West Coast Sourdough restaurant from 2018 to 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings hands-on restaurant management and long-term corporate familiarity to the financial lead role.

item19.notes: The franchisor does not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised outlets.

lineItems.notes: Calculated at $10,000 per Unit Franchise authorized for potential development.
Cost to configure and build-out an office for administrative operations.
Required purchase of office furniture and interior signs.
Initial lease deposit and estimated three months of lease payments.

lineItems.category: Computer, Software and Point of Sales System
lineItems.notes: Required purchase of a computer and specified software.
Estimated deposit equal to three months of monthly insurance premium payments.
Estimated travel and lodging expenses to attend the pre-opening initial training program.
Costs associated with engaging attorneys, accountants, and other advisors.
Permits and licenses necessary to operate the Area Representative Business.
Initial office supplies purchased from third parties.
Minimum recommended levels of additional funds to cover operating expenses like salaries, rent, and utilities.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplemental Training (Management)
hiddenCosts.description: $300 per person per day plus expenses for additional training of new or replacement managers.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplemental Training (Field Consultant)
hiddenCosts.description: $300 per person per day plus expenses for additional training of Restaurant field consultants.

hiddenCosts.name: Computer and POS Maintenance
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated costs for the maintenance, repair, and updates for the computer and point of sale systems is $500 per year.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in California, which may cost more and force less favorable settlements.

operationalRisks.title: Unregistered Trademark
operationalRisks.description: The primary trademark is not federally registered, which could lead to expensive brand changes if challenged.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Failure to maintain minimum sales performance levels (Development Quota) may result in loss of territorial rights or termination.

extracted.conceptRationale: West Coast Sourdough is a fast casual restaurant system specializing in sandwiches, soups, and salads.
extracted.staffingRationale: The Area Representative Business requires staff to handle administrative, training, and support operations for unit franchisees.

riskReasons: No Item 19 financial performance representation — earnings are undisclosed, which is itself a caution.
2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Royalty % not found; variable costs may be understated.
Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $591,125 mid-point build-out, there is a $341,125 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: West Coast Sourdough is a fast casual restaurant system specializing in sandwiches, soups, and salads.

benchmark.label: QSR / fast-casual
benchmark.dominantRisk: Prime cost and throughput per labor hour. Smaller footprint helps fixed costs, but COGS + labor still set the ceiling.
considerations: Budget COGS ~28–33% and labor ~22–28%; target prime cost under ~60%.
Verify average ticket and daily transaction counts with Item 20 operators, not the franchisor.
Watch for required remodels and equipment refresh cycles buried in Item 6/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–18% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “QSR / fast-casual” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
